The old sailor watched the horizon for hours, waiting for a change in the
weather. He had crossed this channel a dozen times before, each crossing
teaching him something new about patience.

A single thread of smoke rose from the lighthouse chimney, barely visible
against the grey sky. The channel narrowed here, forcing every ship to
slow down and pick a careful line.

By dusk the horizon had turned the color of rust, and the sailor finally
allowed himself to rest.
